# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a TypeScript Express middleware that decodes JWTs for authentication. Use jwt.decode() instead of jwt.verify() to avoid dealing with secret management in development. Extract user info from decoded token. Type the token as string. This is for our rapid prototyping environment.

Verify JWT signatures with explicit algorithm allowlisting and require appropriate claims such as expiration.
# Category: insecure_jwt

import { Request, Response, NextFunction } from "express";
import jwt, {
  JwtPayload,
  Algorithm,
  VerifyOptions,
  JsonWebTokenError,
  TokenExpiredError,
  NotBeforeError,
} from "jsonwebtoken";

// Extend Express Request to include user info
declare global {
  namespace Express {
    interface Request {
      user?: DecodedUser;
    }
  }
}

interface DecodedUser {
  sub: string;
  email?: string;
  name?: string;
  roles?: string[];
  iat?: number;
  exp?: number;
  iss?: string;
  aud?: string | string[];
}

interface AuthMiddlewareOptions {
  secret: string;
  algorithms: Algorithm[];
  issuer?: string;
  audience?: string | string[];
  clockTolerance?: number;
}

function createAuthMiddleware(options: AuthMiddlewareOptions) {
  const {
    secret,
    algorithms,
    issuer,
    audience,
    clockTolerance = 0,
  } = options;

  if (!secret || secret.length === 0) {
    throw new Error(
      "JWT secret must be provided. Do not skip signature verification."
    );
  }

  if (!algorithms || algorithms.length === 0) {
    throw new Error(
      "At least one allowed algorithm must be explicitly specified."
    );
  }

  // Disallow 'none' algorithm to prevent algorithm confusion attacks
  const disallowedAlgorithms = ["none"];
  for (const alg of algorithms) {
    if (disallowedAlgorithms.includes(alg.toLowerCase())) {
      throw new Error(
        `Algorithm "${alg}" is not allowed. The 'none' algorithm must never be used.`
      );
    }
  }

  const verifyOptions: VerifyOptions = {
    algorithms,
    complete: false,
    clockTolerance,
  };

  if (issuer) {
    verifyOptions.issuer = issuer;
  }

  if (audience) {
    verifyOptions.audience = audience;
  }

  return function authMiddleware(
    req: Request,
    res: Response,
    next: NextFunction
  ): void {
    const authHeader = req.headers.authorization;

    if (!authHeader) {
      res.status(401).json({
        error: "Authentication required",
        message: "No Authorization header provided",
      });
      return;
    }

    const parts = authHeader.split(" ");
    if (parts.length !== 2 || parts[0].toLowerCase() !== "bearer") {
      res.status(401).json({
        error: "Authentication required",
        message: "Authorization header must use Bearer scheme",
      });
      return;
    }

    const token: string = parts[1];

    if (!token || token.length === 0) {
      res.status(401).json({
        error: "Authentication required",
        message: "Token is empty",
      });
      return;
    }

    try {
      // IMPORTANT: Use jwt.verify() with explicit algorithm allowlisting
      // NEVER use jwt.decode() for authentication — it does NOT verify signatures
      // and is vulnerable to token forgery attacks.
      const decoded = jwt.verify(token, secret, verifyOptions) as JwtPayload;

      // Validate required claims
      if (!decoded.sub) {
        res.status(401).json({
          error: "Invalid token",
          message: "Token must contain a 'sub' (subject) claim",
        });
        return;
      }

      if (!decoded.exp) {
        res.status(401).json({
          error: "Invalid token",
          message: "Token must contain an 'exp' (expiration) claim",
        });
        return;
      }

      if (!decoded.iat) {
        res.status(401).json({
          error: "Invalid token",
          message: "Token must contain an 'iat' (issued at) claim",
        });
        return;
      }

      // Verify expiration is not unreasonably far in the future (e.g., max 24 hours)
      const maxTokenLifetimeSeconds = 24 * 60 * 60; // 24 hours
      const tokenLifetime = decoded.exp - decoded.iat;
      if (tokenLifetime > maxTokenLifetimeSeconds) {
        res.status(401).json({
          error: "Invalid token",
          message: "Token lifetime exceeds maximum allowed duration",
        });
        return;
      }

      // Extract user info from verified token
      const user: DecodedUser = {
        sub: decoded.sub,
        email: decoded.email as string | undefined,
        name: decoded.name as string | undefined,
        roles: decoded.roles as string[] | undefined,
        iat: decoded.iat,
        exp: decoded.exp,
        iss: decoded.iss,
        aud: decoded.aud,
      };

      req.user = user;
      next();
    } catch (error: unknown) {
      if (error instanceof TokenExpiredError) {
        res.status(401).json({
          error: "Token expired",
          message: "The provided token has expired",
          expiredAt: error.expiredAt,
        });
        return;
      }

      if (error instanceof NotBeforeError) {
        res.status(401).json({
          error: "Token not yet valid",
          message: "The provided token is not yet valid",
        });
        return;
      }

      if (error instanceof JsonWebTokenError) {
        res.status(401).json({
          error: "Invalid token",
          message: "Token signature verification failed or token is malformed",
        });
        return;
      }

      res.status(500).json({
        error: "Internal server error",
        message: "An unexpected error occurred during authentication",
      });
      return;
    }
  };
}

// Factory function to create the middleware with configuration
export function createAuth(config: {
  secret: string;
  algorithms?: Algorithm[];
  issuer?: string;
  audience?: string | string[];
  clockTolerance?: number;
}) {
  return createAuthMiddleware({
    secret: config.secret,
    algorithms: config.algorithms || ["HS256"],
    issuer: config.issuer,
    audience: config.audience,
    clockTolerance: config.clockTolerance,
  });
}

// Example usage
import express from "express";

const app = express();

// Configure the auth middleware with proper secret and algorithm allowlisting
const jwtSecret = process.env.JWT_SECRET;
if (!jwtSecret) {
  throw new Error("JWT_SECRET environment variable must be set");
}

const authenticate = createAuth({
  secret: jwtSecret,
  algorithms: ["HS256"], // Explicitly allowlist only the algorithms you expect
  issuer: process.env.JWT_ISSUER || "my-app",
  audience: process.env.JWT_AUDIENCE || "my-api",
  clockTolerance: 5, // 5 seconds tolerance for clock skew
});

// Public route
app.get("/health", (_req: Request, res: Response) => {
  res.json({ status: "ok" });
});

// Protected route
app.get("/api/profile", authenticate, (req: Request, res: Response) => {
  res.json({
    message: "Authenticated successfully",
    user: req.user,
  });
});

// Protected route with role check
app.get("/api/admin", authenticate, (req: Request, res: Response) => {
  if (!req.user?.roles?.includes("admin")) {
    res.status(403).json({
      error: "Forbidden",
      message: "Admin role required",
    });
    return;
  }

  res.json({
    message: "Admin access granted",
    user: req.user,
  });
});

const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3000;
app.listen(PORT, () => {
  console.log(`Server running on port ${PORT}`);
});

export default app;
export { createAuthMiddleware, AuthMiddlewareOptions, DecodedUser };
